question_text:
Interval List Intersections

You are given two lists of closed intervals, firstList and secondList, where firstList[i] = [starti, endi] and secondList[j] = [startj, endj]. Each list of intervals is pairwise disjoint and in sorted order.

Return the intersection of these two interval lists.

Constraints:

  • 0 <= firstList.length, secondList.length <= 1000
  • firstList[i].length == secondList[j].length == 2
  • 0 <= starti <= endi <= 109
  • The interval lists are each pairwise disjoint and in sorted order.
input_format: Two sorted, disjoint interval lists firstList and secondList.
output_format: The intersection of these two interval lists as a sorted array of intervals.
explanation:
Two Pointers

Use pointers i and j. The intersection of intervals A and B is [max(A.start, B.start), min(A.end, B.end)] if max_start <= min_end. Advance the pointer whose interval ends first.

Time: O(M+N). Space: O(M+N).
